These are flexible enough that different orderings, formatting, or exact type annotations would still pass. The parameter just needs to be named 'expect' with a type annotation.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests require actual code changes: removing eslint-disable comments, adding expect parameters to function signatures, replacing expect.fail with throw new Error, and updating call sites. A stub/no-op implementation would fail because the original files contain the patterns being checked against. The pass-to-pass typecheck/unit tests also ensure the changes compile and work correctly.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is available at /workspace/workers-sdk in the container, and the agent could read the solve.sh or the gold patch. The Dockerfile doesn't remove any solution files. Also, since tests are purely text-based regex checks on file contents, the agent could just pattern-match the expected strings without understanding the underlying problem.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors described in the instruction are tested: eslint-disable comment removal/justification, expect parameter injection in all listed functions, mocks.ts throw new Error replacement, call-site updates, plus typecheck/build/unit tests as pass-to-pass checks.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (pass-to-pass vs fail-to-pass), descriptive function names and docstrings, and logical grouping by concern (eslint comments, expect params, mocks, call sites).

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves code refactoring, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: All file paths, function names, and variable names in the instruction match those used in the tests and the actual repository structure.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names every specific file, every specific function, the exact type name (ExpectStatic), the exact replacement pattern (expect.fail -> throw new Error), and even the exact parameter signature pattern (expect: ExpectStatic).
